scSidekick - Co-expression Analysis

16_coexpression.Rmd

Overview

Three functions cover the co-expression workflow end-to-end:

  • CorrelateGene() - correlate one gene of interest against every other gene, within each cell type, at single-cell, pseudobulk, or group-average resolution.
  • PlotCorrelation() - visualize the results as per-cell-type volcano-style plots (r vs. -log10 adjusted p-value).
  • SplitByGene() - label each cell as high / low (or expressed / zero) based on a gene’s expression, per cell type. Feeds directly into RunGSEA_pseudobulk() or FindMarkers().

1. CorrelateGene() - single-cell level

The default level = "sc" computes Spearman correlation across individual cells within each cell type. Only genes expressed in at least min_pct% of cells in a given cell type are tested (expressed_only = TRUE by default).

cor_sc <- CorrelateGene(bmcite,
  gene     = "LYZ",
  group.by = "celltype.l1",
  level    = "sc")

head(cor_sc)

Each row is one gene in one cell type. Key columns:

Column Description
gene Correlated gene
cell_type Cell-type group
r Spearman (or Pearson) correlation with the gene of interest
p Raw p-value
padj BH-adjusted p-value
n Number of cells tested
goi Gene of interest
level Resolution used ("sc", "pseudobulk", or "group")

2. PlotCorrelation() - visualize results

PlotCorrelation() draws one panel per cell type showing the top n_pos positively and top n_neg negatively correlated genes. Significant genes (padj < padj_cutoff) are colored; the top label_n in each direction are labeled. Panels are assembled with patchwork::wrap_plots().

PlotCorrelation(cor_sc,
  n_pos       = 20,
  n_neg       = 20,
  label_n     = 5,
  padj_cutoff = 0.05,
  r_cutoff    = 0.3,     # draws ±0.3 dotted reference lines
  ncol        = 3)

Restrict to specific cell types with cell_type:

PlotCorrelation(cor_sc,
  cell_type = c("NK", "Mono"),
  n_pos     = 15,
  n_neg     = 15,
  label_n   = 6)

Save to PDF (a .legend sidecar is written automatically):

PlotCorrelation(cor_sc,
  n_pos      = 20,
  n_neg      = 20,
  output_dir = "./Figures",
  file_name  = "LYZ_coexpression")

3. CorrelateGene() - pseudobulk level

For multi-donor datasets, pseudobulk correlation (level = "pseudobulk") aggregates counts to the donor x cell-type level first. This removes within-donor cell-count inflation and is more appropriate for multi-sample comparisons. Requires sample_column.

cor_pb <- CorrelateGene(bmcite,
  gene          = "LYZ",
  group.by      = "celltype.l1",
  level         = "pseudobulk",
  sample_column = "donor",
  min_donors    = 3)

Group-average level

level = "group" averages expression within each combination of cell type and group_column, then correlates across those group-level averages. Useful when donors are few but groups are many (e.g. comparing many cell-type x timepoint combinations).

cor_grp <- CorrelateGene(bmcite,
  gene         = "LYZ",
  group.by     = "celltype.l1",
  level        = "group",
  group_column = "donor",
  min_groups   = 3)

4. Stratified analysis with compare_by

Pass compare_by to run the correlation separately within each level of a grouping variable (e.g. sex, condition, batch). The result gains a compare_group column, and PlotCorrelation() automatically lays out one column per comparison group.

cor_by_donor <- CorrelateGene(bmcite,
  gene       = "LYZ",
  group.by   = "celltype.l1",
  level      = "sc",
  compare_by = "donor")

# ncol defaults to the number of comparison groups
PlotCorrelation(cor_by_donor,
  cell_type = "Mono",
  n_pos     = 15,
  n_neg     = 15)

5. SplitByGene() - label cells for downstream analysis

SplitByGene() adds a new metadata column splitting cells into expression groups within each cell type. The column plugs straight into RunGSEA_pseudobulk() (as contrast.by) or Seurat::FindMarkers().

Default: zero vs. expressed 

bmcite <- SplitByGene(bmcite,
  gene     = "LYZ",
  group.by = "celltype.l1",
  split    = "zero_vs_expressed")

# New column "LYZ_split" is now in meta.data:
table(bmcite$LYZ_split, bmcite$celltype.l1, useNA = "ifany")

Quantile split 

bmcite <- SplitByGene(bmcite,
  gene           = "LYZ",
  group.by       = "celltype.l1",
  split          = "quantile",
  quantile_range = c(0.25, 0.75))  # bottom 25% = "low", top 25% = "high"

Middle cells (between the two quantiles) receive NA and are excluded from downstream tests, keeping only the contrast extremes.

Feeding into RunGSEA_pseudobulk() 

RunGSEA_pseudobulk(bmcite,
  group.by    = "celltype.l1",
  sample.by   = "donor",
  contrast.by = "LYZ_split",
  contrasts   = list(expressed_vs_zero = "expressed - zero"),
  species     = "human",
  output_dir  = "./Figures/LYZ_pseudobulk_GSEA")

Feeding into FindMarkers() 

mono <- subset(bmcite, celltype.l1 == "Mono")

markers <- Seurat::FindMarkers(mono,
  group.by  = "LYZ_split",
  ident.1   = "expressed",
  ident.2   = "zero",
  min.pct   = 0.1,
  logfc.threshold = 0.25)

head(markers)

See also

  • Vignette 05: GroupHeatmap(), SplitDotPlot() - expression summaries per cell type
  • Vignette 08: RunGSEA() - pathway analysis on a gene list
  • Vignette 09: RunGSEA_pseudobulk() - full pseudobulk GSEA pipeline (contrast.by accepts the column from SplitByGene)

Session info 

sessionInfo()
## R version 4.3.1 (2023-06-16)
## Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin20 (64-bit)
## Running under: macOS 15.6
## 
## Matrix products: default
